title: Memory, Storage, RAID, and Removable Media · Support Workbench
summary: A guided support bench extends memory and storage integrity decision record while retaining authorization, safety, baseline, privacy, accessibility, rollback, verification, documentation, and user communication. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of memory and storage integrity decision record while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare RAM generation, form factor, capacity, channels, speed, timing, voltage, ECC, registered status, compatibility, and population rules.
Distinguish capacity pressure, application faults, thermal instability, compatibility, contact, and hardware errors using repeatable evidence.
Compare magnetic, flash, optical, removable, SATA, SAS, NVMe, form factor, interface, endurance, latency, throughput, and workload fit.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Workshop Ram Properties
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Crashes, storage pressure, a degraded array, and a healthy-looking drive blur capacity, hardware, filesystem, redundancy, backup, and custody boundaries. The workshop variation 4 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Compare RAM generation, form factor, capacity, channels, speed, timing, voltage, ECC, registered status, compatibility, and population rules.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Workshop Ram Properties.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for compare ram generation, form factor, capacity, channels, speed, timing, voltage, ecc, registered status, compatibility, and population rules. For CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Workshop Ram Properties, keep Ram Properties connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

steps.instruction:
workshop case — Crashes, storage pressure, a degraded array, and a healthy-looking drive blur capacity, hardware, filesystem, redundancy, backup, and custody boundaries. The workshop variation 2 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Preserve data custody while assessing capacity, SMART or vendor health, filesystem state, encryption, backups, safe removal, replacement, and disposition.

One precondition fails while output still looks plausible. What is the correct response?

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Storage Health Custody.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for preserve data custody while assessing capacity, smart or vendor health, filesystem state, encryption, backups, safe removal, replacement, and disposition. For CompTIA A+ support Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Storage Health Custody, keep Storage Health Custody connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

stimulus.title: Storage Health Custody failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: A drive health percentage is a complete diagnosis and exact lifetime forecast.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Preserve data custody while assessing capacity, SMART or vendor health, filesystem state, encryption, backups, safe removal, replacement, and disposition.

options.text: For test an acceptance boundary, apply one familiar repair, omit user impact, authorization, safety, asset and version identity, baseline, recent change, competing cause, prediction, observation, rollback, changed-case verification, accessibility, privacy, escalation, and controlled transfer, then accept a missing symptom as support proof.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Storage Health Custody condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim fails the evidence boundary for Storage Health Custody.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Preserve data custody while assessing capacity, SMART or vendor health, filesystem state, encryption, backups, safe removal, replacement, and disposition.
